detail: shipped MyTester skeleton: coverage-driven single-test-writer, one Engine call (NoopEngine->placeholder), Workspace worktree + Guard-checked git/gh, kind=run ledger; ruff+pytest green locally (5 tests)

extract the unit's source via ast.get_source_segment, not inspect.getsource — avoids importing arbitrary target-repo code (side effects, missing deps) just to introspect a function; the doc's inspect suggestion was an implementation hint, ast is the robust equivalent

coverage_after is recorded as null in v0 rather than recomputed — the NoopEngine placeholder test adds no real coverage, so claiming an after-number would be dishonest; recompute once a real Engine writes meaningful tests

my-tester depends on my-guard as an installed package (first tool->tool runtime dep), per the design doc's 'MyGuard for Policy'; CI installs both mythings-core and my-guard from git@main like the existing sibling-install pattern

measure_coverage injects the worktree (and worktree/src) onto PYTHONPATH so a flat or src-layout target package is importable without installing it; keeps MyTester runtime dependency-free while shelling out to the target's own pytest-cov
